======= RMM08420White collection of historical medallions8420Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

euro finartThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:09:17 UTC.12/07/2018

English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
White collection of historical medallions
White, Andrew Dickson, 1832-1918
8420
8 cubic feet. 8 cubic feet.
1868. Medallions "The White Collection of Historical Medallions, including, among other things, all the casts of Eichler of Berlin, namely two thousand copies of antique gems in the Royal Museum at Berlin, illustrative of ancient history and art; over five hundred casts illustrating mediaeval and renaissance history and art; some four hundred medallion portraits of eminent personages in modern history, derived from authentic portraits." Cornell University Register, 1868-69. Collection material in English 8040774
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2015
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2015
Date modified:
RMC Staff, February 2016
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
"The White Collection of Historical Medallions, including, among other things, all the casts of Eichler of Berlin, namely two thousand copies of antique gems in the Royal Museum at Berlin, illustrative of ancient history and art; over five hundred casts illustrating mediaeval and renaissance history and art; some four hundred medallion portraits of eminent personages in modern history, derived from authentic portraits." Cornell University Register, 1868-69.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Group 1: Unlabelled Medallions
Series II. Group II: Von Stosch Seal Collection
Series III. Group III: Italian Gems
Series IV. Loose Medallions from A.D. White Library
Glass Bound Medallions
Large Medallions
Medium Medallions
Small Medallions
Cite As:
White Collection of Historical Medallions, #8420. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access restricted to the permission of the curator.
Glyptics. Sculpture, Classical. Plaster casts.

Scope and Contents

Scope and Contents
66-121: Stones with Oriental characters, cut stones from recent timesGroup 3: Italian Gems
Scope and Contents
These gems were made in Rome between 1829 and 1836, and were bought by A.D. White in Berlin in 1881. The original handwritten catalogue of these gems is in collection #1-2-2, box 206.Classical gems 1-10019AClassical gems 101-20019BClassical gems 201-30020CClassical gems 301-40020DLoose Medallions
Scope and Contents
These medallions were previously stored in the A.D. White library in Uris Library. A handwritten list of some of the medallions can be found in the George Lincoln Burr papers, #14-17-22, Box 1 Folder 5.Glass Bound Medallions
Scope and Contents
15 centimeters in diameter.Head of Christ, by Carlo Dolci21Christ with Crown of Thorns, by Guido Reni21Madonna del Lago, by Raphael21Madonna della Sedia, by Raphael21Christ blessing the children, by Thorvaldsen21Mary with Christ and John, by Thorvaldsen21Christmas Eve in Heaven, by Thorvaldsen21Spring, by Thorvaldsen21Summer, by Thorvaldsen21Autumn, by Thorvaldsen21Winter, by Thorvaldsen21Genius of the Year, by Thorvaldsen21Architecture, by Thorvaldsen22Painting, by Thorvaldsen22Sculpture, by Thorvaldsen22Poetry, by Thorvaldsen22Music and Poesy, by Thorvaldsen22Morning, by Thorvaldsen22Tragedy and Comedy, by Thorvaldsen22Amor and Hymen, by Thorvaldsen22Hercules and Hebe, by Thorvaldsen22Hygeia and Aesculapius, by Thorvaldsen22Schwebende Engel, by Thorvaldsen22Musicirende Engel, by Thorvaldsen22Ariosto, Chamisso, Dante, Goethe, Herder, Lessing, and Liszt23Meyerbeer, Romano, Guilio, Schiller, Tasso, and Titian23Wieland24Large Medallions
Scope and Contents
Circa 11-13 centimeters in diameterUntitled bust, with "Philis Coespa Reni" around the edge24Pisani Pictoris Opvs, with bird images24Opvs Constanti, labelled number 20 on back24Labelled Wilbalden von Redwiz, 153624Anne of Bretagne24Louis XII24Albert of Bavaria, labelled number 1124Venator Intrepidus, Alfonso V24Giovanni Bentivoglio II24Parcere subiectis et debellare superbos, with two lions24Nvnc Deniqve Vives24Bust of Christ24Pope Paul II bust24John the Baptist24Nicolas de Neufville24Victor Amadeus of Savoy24Cristina de Lorena24John VIII Palaeologus24Guilio Romano24Dr. Johann Klainmuller24Sultan Mohammet, 148124Alfonso V of Aragon27Maria de Medicis27Philibert II of Savoy and Margaret of Austria27Christ27Lorenzo de Medici27Pisani Opvs, with gryphon27Sigimundus de Malatestis27Man on a horse, with the back labelled Ludwig (Ludovicus) Gonzaga27Alfonso V of Aragon27Man on a horse outside a castle, with the back labelled Sigimundus de Malatestis27Philipp Maria Sforza, 1479, with reverse27Benedictvs Depastis, with back labelled Guarinus27Nonina Stroza27Marchio Leonellus Este, with reverse27Ludovicus Gonzaga27Sultan Mohameti27Sultan Mohamed II27Two men on horses27Johannes Tornabonus27Gvarinvs Vcronensis27Johannes Franciscus de Gonzaga27Alfonso of Aragon27Medium Medallions
Scope and Contents
3-7 centimeters in diameter132 portraits, European2580 portraits, European2626 portraits, European28
Scope and Contents
All marked with a blue 'x' on the backIoannes Maria Pomedellvs2823 portraits, European28
Scope and Contents
With blue writing on back83 portraits, some with writing on the back29Portraits: Germany, Prussia, Saxony, France, England, Poland30Portraits: Poland, Russia, Sweden, Popes, Denmark, Spain and Italy31Portraits: Spain and Italy, smaller German states,32Miscellaneous portraits33Miscellaneous portraits34Miscellaneous portraits35Miscellaneous portraits36Miscellaneous portraits37Miscellaneous portraits38Miscellaneous portraits39Miscellaneous portraits40Miscellaneous portraits41Small Medallions

1-3 centimeters in diameter9 portraits28
